mentions.note: Trading at 47x earnings; buy some here, hope for pullback to 45x. Ladder in rather than chasing. Worst case opens up to $1025 and you exit.
Club retailer divergence winner; red hot momentum; preferred long-run over BJ's which lags; solid long-term positioning.
Keeper of low-price bargain club; $1.50 hot dog combo sacrosanct even with lower gasoline; won't let inflation hurt customers; among retail winners today as consumers seek value.
Sells at 49x earnings (never cheap, never ever cheap, always terrific); would not buy right here; best for long-term holders; one of longest owning stocks for charitable trust.
Cramer holds for charitable trust; company planning to pass tariff refunds back to members. Position for Costco reflects confidence in member loyalty strategy.
Nico's position; rolling out more gasoline stations; cheap gas drives membership signups and card sales; good business model.
Two catalysts: cheapest gasoline in country attracts card signups (where profit is made); inflation-fighter positioning; inflation expected to return.
Down; retail weakness signal; expects stronger leadership in rally.

THE stock to buy in this difficult market environment alongside Walmart; represents difficult economic conditions; new CEO in place; stay long; won't see further major pullbacks; liked more than ever.
Up 12% YTD; high multiples but defensive quality; hold if concerned about slowdown, though valuation is extended.
MVP (Most Value for Price) identified by 100X data; bargain model; creates customer value at given price point; consistent outperformer.
Hold for new highs; solid earnings recently; historically good entry point when gasoline spikes; wait for pullback before adding more.
Special place in Cramer's heart; key metric is membership card renewal rate, which has declined; younger people drift to e-commerce, don't know bargains.
Value-oriented wholesale retailer; straightforward business model; benefits from rotation to understandable, in-demand businesses.
Up 3% on broader retail rally and strong economic data; defensive quality retailer gaining on PMI strength.

Trading 50x earnings (vs. 47x historical high); re-up rate weaker than desired but fundamentals coming through. Stay long; recent bounce technical, not fundamental. Pantry-stocking catalyst already discounted.

Down 20% from June highs; Larry Williams cycle analysis shows undervalued setup with 85% historical rally rate from this point; projects 5-month uptrend into late Feb then June; professional accumulation strong despite price decline.
